questions.question: To satisfy medical requirements, all sUAS crewmembers must ...

choices.text: be free of any physical or mental condition that would interfere with the safe operation of the small unmanned aircraft.
hold a valid third-class medical certificate.
complete a physical with an Aviation Medical Examiner.

questions.question: Which of the following would medically disqualify a Remote Pilot?

choices.text: A migraine headache with the side effect of blurred vision
Taking a presciption medication that does not have any noticeable side effects

questions.question: Who is responsible for determining whether a pilot is fit to fly for a particular flight?

You have been hired as a Remote Pilot in Command by a local TV news station to film breaking news with a small unmanned aircraft. You expressed a safety concern and the station manager has instructed you to "fly first, ask questions later." What type of hazardous attitude does this attitude represent?
What antidotal phrase can help reverse the hazardous attitude of impulsivity?

choices.text: Do it quickly to get it over with.

questions.question: What would most likely result in hyperventilation?

choices.text: as being in an impaired state
by an abiliity to overcome sleep deprivation

questions.question: Safety is an important element for a remote pilot to consider prior to operating an unmanned aircraft system. To prevent the final "link" in the accident chain, a remote pilot must consider which methodology?
A remote pilot in command and a visual observer define their roles and responsibilities prior to and during the operation of a small UA. This is a good use of ...

You are operating near the Fremont Airport, and monitoring air traffic radio for enhanced safety. A pilot reports their position as being on the left downwind leg for runway 32. Where is that aircraft in relation to the airfield?
